title: Write-Host in module functions bypasses pipeline and redirection
description: Write-Host writes directly to the host console, bypassing the PowerShell pipeline. Output cannot be captured, redirected, piped, or tested. In module functions, this prevents callers from processing output programmatically. Write-Output, Write-Verbose, or Write-Information should be used instead. Write-Host is acceptable in interactive scripts (UI rendering, progress display, console formatting) but should be flagged in reusable module functions that may be called by automation or tests.
detection_method: Regex scan for Write-Host calls in .psm1 module files (excluding comment lines)
preventionRule: In module functions, use Write-Output for data, Write-Verbose for diagnostics, Write-Information for host messages that support redirection. Reserve Write-Host for interactive UI-only contexts.
remedy: Replace Write-Host with Write-Verbose (for debug/diagnostic), Write-Output (for data), or Write-Information (for host info that supports -InformationAction). Keep Write-Host only for UI rendering like progress bars and interactive prompts.
